stories.title: Marysville eyes one-year freeze on new data centers
stories.dek: City leaders want to pause proposals while they study power and water demands. Residents 30 minutes away could see higher utility bills if large projects move in unchecked. The move would give local officials time to write zoning rules before any deals are signed.
stories.source_title: City wants data center moratorium - The Marysville Advocate

stories.title: Saline Township caps Oracle tax break at $4.8B after rejecting bigger cut
stories.dek: Trustees approved a limited tax abatement for the Oracle campus but refused a deeper 50% break that would have cost local schools and services far more. The $4.8 billion cap keeps the original project size in play while protecting township revenue. Residents 30 minutes away will feel the difference in property-tax bills and future power rates.
stories.source_title: Saline Township board approves Oracle tax abatement, cap it at original $4.8B project cost - WXYZ 7 News Detroit

stories.title: Pere Marquette eyes year-long data center freeze
stories.dek: Township leaders are debating a moratorium that would block new proposals while residents weigh impacts on local power supply and roads. A pause would give officials time to study water use and tax effects before any large project moves forward. Mason County families could see decisions that shape growth for years.
stories.source_title: Pere Marquette Township weighs data center moratorium - Shoreline Media Group

stories.title: Wixom residents get say on new data center rules tonight
stories.dek: Wixom city council meets to hear public input on a proposed ordinance governing data centers. Residents near potential sites worry about noise, traffic and strain on local power. The vote could set the ground rules for any future projects in this Oakland County suburb.
stories.source_title: Wixom residents to weigh in on data center ordinance as city council meets Tuesday night - WXYZ 7 News Detroit

stories.title: Storms Topple Dozens of Warren Power Lines, Fueling Data Center Pause Push
stories.dek: Council Secretary Mindy Moore requested a July 14 moratorium vote after the city's aging grid failed; if passed, Warren would join other Michigan towns pausing projects while it writes zoning rules.

stories.title: Jackson halts all new data centers after council vote
stories.dek: City leaders approved a pause on fresh proposals to study power and water impacts first. Residents nearby worry about noise, traffic, and higher utility bills if multiple projects land at once. The move gives Jackson time to set rules before developers lock in sites.
stories.source_title: Jackson City Council votes to pause new data center development in Jackson - AOL.com

stories.title: Hyperscale developer faces Benton Harbor residents over noise and traffic
stories.dek: The company presented plans at a city council meeting but neighbors still question the scale. Local roads and the electric system were not built for constant heavy truck traffic and round-the-clock power draw. The session showed how quickly community pushback can grow once details surface.
stories.source_title: Hyperscale Data Center addresses community concerns at city council meeting - The Herald Palladium

stories.title: Monroe voters reject data center plan after energy and noise fears
stories.dek: The Monroe Times reports local opposition prevailed in blocking the proposed facility over concerns about power demand and industrial noise. Residents living within a few miles of the site say the project would have raised their utility bills without delivering meaningful tax relief. The decision keeps the county's zoning rules unchanged for now.
stories.source_title: Data Center a big ‘no’ - The Monroe Times

stories.title: Salem Township developer pitches revised data-center plan to ease concerns
stories.dek: The company claims the new design will use less visible equipment and lower noise levels than earlier versions. Neighbors remain skeptical that the promised improvements will hold once construction begins. Washtenaw County residents now watch to see whether local rules can force real changes or just green-light the project.
stories.source_title: Developer promises 'a better data center' in revealing Salem project - The Business Journals

stories.title: Benson under fire for Big Tech cash while data centers multiply
stories.dek: The Michigan official has accepted donations from companies racing to build data centers across the state. Critics say the money undercuts any push to protect residents from higher power costs and land conflicts. The donations spotlight the growing political stakes around these projects.
stories.source_title: How Can Benson Stand Up to Big Tech When She Takes Their Money? - Michigan Enjoyer

stories.title: Lansing council votes six-month freeze on data centers
stories.dek: Lansing leaders approved a half-year pause on new data-center proposals to buy time for rules that protect residents. The move halts projects in the capital while officials weigh power, noise and tax impacts. It marks the latest city to hit the brakes on rapid expansion.
stories.source_title: Lansing City Council passes 6-month data center pause - Yahoo

national.title: Data centers advance rapidly as regulators scramble for rules
national.dek: Across the U.S., officials race to impose guardrails on power-hungry facilities while communities push back on incentives and impacts.

meetings.topic: Informational session on proposed University of Michigan/Los Alamos computational data center project
Review of zoning ordinance including data center definitions and regulations
Data center ordinance amendment for future development regulations
Informational session on University of Michigan/Los Alamos data center project updates, including history, legislative developments, and Q&A
